document.title: Ink Study: Learning the Weight of Water
document.description: Experimenting with blurred edges, reclaimed paper, and the rhythm of washes.

body.raw:
## Palette

- Walnut ink
- Indigo powder
- Rice paper rescued from the recycling stack

The page buckles when it dries and that is half the charm. I layer washes, lift pigment with salt, then leave the mistakes visible so the piece keeps breathing.

> Each bloom is a timestamp of the brush pausing for breath.

document.title: Morning Pages from the Rooftop
document.description: Watching the city wake up while sketching words into motion.

body.raw:
Sometime around sunrise the skyline turns watercolor. I bring a notebook, a mug, and no agenda.

## Things I keep returning to

1. Pencil lines that wobble because my hand is cold.
2. Stories told in soft gradients.
3. Letting drafts stay drafts until the words feel patient.

*"Art isn't what I write, it's the way I listen first."*

document.title: Tracing the Shape of Code
document.description: Sketching an architecture-first approach to building personal sites.

body.raw:
# Tracing the Shape of Code

Designing a personal site starts with the constraints. For this build, the north star is a static export that still feels alive.

> Static-first does not mean lifeless—it means intentional about what truly needs to run on the client.

## Notes from the planning sketch

- Contentlayer owns the data model so we keep MDX portable.
- Light/dark palettes stay in CSS custom properties.
- Keep every fancy animation behind `prefers-reduced-motion` checks.[^motion]

```tsx
export function loadPosts() {
  return sortByDate(allPosts);
}
```

[^motion]: We owe accessibility more than a quick toggle.
